title: High definition secure playback with downloadable drm for android platforms
abstract: A Trustonic DRM Plug-in is provided that can be downloaded and operate in conjunction with an Android framework. The solution also includes a PVP with the downloadable DRM. The system includes components that can be added by Trustonic based on the Android 4.3 Framework in addition to current t-base 300 that can be used by any DRM vendor. The system enables the DRM to be downloaded in the field since all DRMs could use the standard API services of the Android 4.3 OS. With a codec component employed like H.264 or HEVC that can use the PVP with the downloaded DRM component, the Android video player can use the component to satisfy HD content security requirements.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What Is Claimed: 1. A method of providing secure video coding comprising: providing downloadable Digital Rights Media (DRM) components that provide for decryption of video; providing the DRM components so that they interface and are supported by an Android platform Application Program Interface (API); wherein the API renders encrypted elementary streams, including HEVC, that can be decrypted by a Media Crypto Plug-in to the API in conjunction with decryption by the DRM components; wherein the DRM components further interface with a Media Codec; and providing a Protected Video Path (PVP) enhancement for the Media Codec and the Media Crypto Plug-in to enable an Android hardware chip supporting the Android platform API to use the PVP. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein Android platform includes the Android 4.3, 4.4, 5.X and 6.X media framework. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the DRM components, the Media Codec and the Media Crypto support SD, HD and 4K content. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the Media Crypto provides compressed video frames that are protected from tampering and theft. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the DRM components are provided in addition to current t-base 300 in hardware of the video codec. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the Media Crypto plug-in supports DASH AES-CBC, CENC, CBCS and Apple Sample encryption formats. 7. A method of rendering video content comprising: passing encrypted samples from video content comprising HLS TS segments or DASH segments to a Media Crypto with a Crypto Plug-in MIME type; instantiating by the Media Crypto a t-Crypto plugin and calling a decryption process for the samples; sending a decryption command to decrypt the encrypted samples from the t-Crypto plugin to a SM-DRM Trustlet, wherein the encrypted content is stored in a shared memory; and invoking, by the SM-DRM Trustlet, the t-DRM API for decrypting, decoding and rendering of the video content. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the SM-DRM Trustlet and the t-DRM API are DRM components that interface and are supported by an Android platform Application Program Interface (API), wherein the Android platform includes the Android 4.3, 4.4, 5.X and 6.X media framework. 9. The method of claim 7, wherein the t-crypto plug-in supports DASH AES-CBC, CENC, CBCS and Apple Sample encryption formats. 10. A method of obtaining a provisioning key comprising: passing by a Media DRM a request to a t-DRM Plugin translating by the t-DRM Plugin the requests to commands and sending them to a SM Trustlet through a TAC; getting by the t-DRM Plugin the requests from the SM Trustlet; creating by the t-DRM Plugin a Media DRM session and receiving a session ID; providing by the t-DRM plugin the request to the Media DRM; providing by the Media DRM the response to the t-DRM Plug-in passing by the t-DRM Plugin the responses to the SM Trustlet through the TAC to process; obtaining via the TAC a provision key request command that is then provided to a Secure Media DRM (SM-DRM) Trustlet; generating in the SM-DRM Trustlet the provision key request based on the key request command; sending by the SM-DRM Trustlet the key request command to the t-DRM Plugin via the TAC; sending by the t-DRM Plugin the provision key request response to the SM-DRM Trustlet; and processing the key response provided by the t-DRM Plugin to generate the key ID and saving the key ID in secure storage. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the Media DRM, SM-DRM Trustlet and the t-DRM Plugin are DRM components that interface and are supported by an Android platform Application Program Interface (API), wherein the Android platform includes the Android 4.3, 4.4, 5.X and 6.X media framework. 12. A method of providing a secure video playback comprising: calling by a Secure Codec Interface a t-crypto Plugin to provide decryption; sending a decrypt command to the SM-DRM trustlet by the t-crypto Plugin; invoking, by the t-Crypto Plugin the SM-DRM Trustlet decrypt command (CMD); retrieving by the SM-DRM Trustlet a correct key from a Secure Storage; invoking by the SM-DRM Trustlet a t-Play DRM API process; decrypting by the t-Play DRM API using secure buffers the video media; providing the decrypted video media through a Secure Buffer to a Codec; and rendering by the codec the video media. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the M-DRM Trustlet and the t-play DRM are DRM components that interface and are supported by an Android platform Application Program Interface (API), wherein the Android platform includes the Android 4.3, 4.4, 5.X and 6.X media framework. 14. The method of claim 12, wherein the t-crypto Plugin supports DASH AES-CBC, CENC, CBCS and Apple Sample encryption formats.
